seoTitle: Embed Video in a HubSpot Email: How to Add It
metaDescription: Add clickable video thumbnails to HubSpot marketing emails using the Video module, since raw video files can't play directly in most inboxes.
intro: Most email clients block raw video from playing inside the inbox, so HubSpot uses a clickable thumbnail with a play button that links out to your hosted video instead. This guide covers finding the Video module in the email editor, connecting a source like YouTube, Vimeo, or HubSpot Video, and customizing the thumbnail overlay. It ends with checking your click tracking before sending.

steps.heading: Open your email draft
steps.body: Go to Marketing, then Email, and select your active campaign draft to open the drag-and-drop canvas.

steps.heading: Find the Video module
steps.body: Look in the left content module tray for the dedicated Video element.

steps.heading: Drop it into your layout
steps.body: Drag and drop the Video module into your desired email section.

steps.heading: Add your video source
steps.body: Paste a YouTube or Vimeo URL, or select an asset from your HubSpot Video library.

steps.heading: Customize the thumbnail
steps.body: Adjust the preview image, alt text, and play button overlay to fit your branding.

steps.heading: Test and verify click paths
steps.body: Save your changes, run a test email preview, and confirm the video link works correctly.

faq.question: Can video actually play inside a HubSpot email?
faq.answer: No, embedding raw video breaks across most mail clients, so HubSpot uses a clickable thumbnail linking to a hosted video instead.

faq.question: What video sources can I connect to the Video module?
faq.answer: You can link YouTube, Vimeo, custom server URLs, or HubSpot Video, which is powered by Vidyard.

faq.question: Can I customize the play button and thumbnail?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust the thumbnail preview, alt text, and play button overlay aesthetics.

faq.question: How do I track clicks on my embedded video?
faq.answer: Setting up tracking parameters on the video link lets you monitor click-through data and optimize conversions.
